zh-CN.title: Clawdbot：自托管个人AI助手的崛起之路
zh-CN.description: 深度解析 Clawdbot 如何在三个月内从兴趣项目爆火至 51k stars，以及它如何让 AI 真正为你干活
zh-CN.content:
TL;DR: 探索在 3 个月内斩获 51k+ GitHub stars 的顶级个人自托管 AI Agent：Clawdbot。本文独家深挖了这只机器龙虾为何爆火出圈、它的系统权限控制边界（Gateways/Nodes）以及如何在 Telegram、Discord 中一键全端唤醒并完成本地重执行任务带来的未来助理级体验。

最近 AI Agent 圈子里最火的项目之一就是 Clawdbot（小爪机器人，吉祥物是一只太空龙虾 🦞），由知名开发者 Peter Steinberger（@steipete，前 PSPDFKit 创始人）个人搞出来的开源项目。这一趋势与 Antigravity Agent 自治研发体系 有异曲同工之妙，但 Clawdbot 面向的是个人日程与终端生产力。

从 2025 年 11 月 24 日第一个 commit，到现在 2026 年 1 月 27 日，GitHub 已经冲到 51k stars、6.1k forks，开发非常活跃（最近一周几乎每天都有 commit）。它为什么这么火？简单一句话：它让大模型真正”干活”了，而不是只聊天。

今天我们来完整拆解这个项目，帮大家快速了解 Clawdbot 的来龙去脉、核心架构、为什么爆火，以及随之而来的”甜蜜的烦恼”。

它到底解决了什么问题？

现在的 AI 助手大概分两类：

  • 云端聊天型（ChatGPT、Gemini、Claude 网页版）：推理很强，但没法持久记忆，也很难真正操作你的电脑和账号
  • 手机/电脑原生助手（Siri、小爱、Google Assistant）：能做点事，但模型弱、生态封闭、隐私堪忧

Clawdbot 把两者优点合起来了：用最强的前沿模型（推荐 Claude Opus 4.5、GPT 系列、Ollama 本地模型），给你完整工具链，能操作你的真实电脑和账号，而且全部自托管、本地优先，数据不离设备。

最杀手级的体验是：你直接在 Telegram、WhatsApp、Discord、Slack、Signal、iMessage 等你本来就在用的聊天软件里跟它对话。不用额外打开新 App。

核心架构一目了然

Clawdbot 采用非常清晰的模块化设计：

Gateway（网关）

唯一控制中枢，默认绑定本地回环 ws://127.0.0.1:18789，负责：

  • 所有客户端的 WebSocket 连接
  • 各种聊天渠道的接入
  • 工具调用分发
  • 会话管理、记忆、定时任务、Webhook
  • Canvas 可视化工作区（http://...:18793/__clawdbot__/canvas/）

Node（节点）

平台专属运行时，负责需要本地权限的操作，比如 macOS 的屏幕录制、摄像头、通知、shell 命令等。

Channels（渠道）

输入输出的桥梁，目前支持 50+ 平台，包括 WhatsApp（扫码登录)、Telegram（Bot Token）、Discord、Slack、Signal、iMessage（仅 macOS）、Matrix、Zalo 等。

Tools & Skills（工具与技能）

真正干活的部分：

  • 内置：浏览器自动化（专用 Chrome CDP）、Canvas 视觉工作区、系统操作、定时任务等
  • 可扩展：通过 ClawdHub（社区技能中心）安装别人写的技能，还支持 AI 自己生成技能并热加载
  • 安全隔离：主会话（你自己）有完整主机权限；群聊/陌生人会话强制走 Docker sandbox（严格的白名单/黑名单）

模型支持

Anthropic（强烈推荐 Opus 4.5）、OpenAI、本地 Ollama 等，支持路由、故障转移。

工作流程

整个流程超级优雅：

你 → 在ChatBox发消息 → Channel → Gateway → AI 思考 + 工具调用 → 执行（主机或沙箱） → 回复到你ChatBox

快速上手 

1
2
3
4
5

# 需要 Node.js ≥ 22
npm install -g clawdbot@latest

# 启动向导，安装成系统服务
clawdbot onboard --install-daemon

向导会一步步带你：

  1. 配置模型 API Key
  2. 登录聊天渠道（扫码或输入 Token）
  3. 授予 macOS 必要权限（屏幕录制等）
  4. 创建第一个工作空间

几分钟后，你就可以在任意绑定的聊天软件里 @ 它了。

为什么这么快就火了？又为什么会出问题？

火的原因

  • 一行命令安装 + 真能干活（清邮箱、航班值机、浏览器操作、日历管理）
  • “未来 Siri” 的感觉：主动、语音唤醒、多渠道、实时画布
  • Peter 本人影响力 + 公开用 Claude 快速开发的开发日志
  • 社区爆发：Discord 几千人、ClawdHub 技能市场、各种教程

经典开源困境

但火得太快也带来了问题：

  • 非技术用户：看到推文/视频觉得很酷 → 冲动安装 → 遇到配置复杂、bug、权限问题 → 各种吐槽
  • 安全研究者：看到 50k+ star 的热门项目 → 扫描漏洞 → 发现有人误配 Tailscale/端口转发暴露 Gateway → 报告并要 bug bounty
  • 期望错位：很多人把它当成熟商业产品用，却忽略这是个不到 3 个月的 beta 项目，主要由 Peter 一人维护（加社区 PR），赞助勉强够买个 Mac Mini

Peter 最近发推吐槽：免费兴趣项目被当成大厂产品伺候，收到一堆企业级需求和安全报告。私信和邮件压力很大。

社区反应很暖：公开回复几乎全是鼓励”继续搞”“屏蔽噪音”“有人愿意赞助硬件”。

官方安全提醒

来自文档和最近的 commit：

  • Gateway 默认只绑本地回环，严禁直接公网绑定
  • 远程访问用 Tailscale + 密码认证
  • 陌生人 DM 默认”配对模式”，需要手动批准
  • 非主会话强制 Docker sandbox
  • 运行 clawdbot doctor 检查风险配置

给 2026 年开源 AI 项目的几点启示

  1. 病毒式增长是双刃剑：带来海量用户，也带来海量支持压力
  2. 能力越强，风险越大：全系统权限很爽，但一旦配置出错后果严重
  3. 文档警告再醒目，很多人还是会忽略（尤其是 demo 太炫）
  4. 单人维护者 burnout 风险极高：社区贡献、安全审计、明确标注”beta、非技术用户慎装”能缓解，但治标不治本

想玩的同学（建议有技术背景）

  • 仓库：https://github.com/clawdbot/clawdbot
  • 文档：https://docs.clawd.bot
  • Discord 社区：https://discord.gg/clawd（非常活跃）

推荐在干净机器上先试，优先用 Anthropic Opus 4.5。先读安全页。遇到问题请友好提 issue 或 PR。

这只龙虾还年轻，但已经让很多人重新思考”个人 AI 助手应该长什么样”。

你会愿意跑一个有完整主机权限的本地 Agent 吗？还是更倾向严格沙箱？欢迎留言交流～ 🦞

en.title: Clawdbot: The Rise of Self-Managed Personal AI Assistants
en.description: In-depth analysis of how Clawdbot went from a hobby project to a viral sensation with 51k stars in just three months, and how it truly makes AI work for you.
en.content:
TL;DRExplore the top-performing self-hosted AI agent that garnered 51k+ GitHub stars in 3 months:ClawdbotThis article provides an exclusive in-depth look at why this robotic lobster has become so popular, its system permission control boundaries (Gateways/Nodes), and how it delivers a future-oriented assistant-like experience by being able to wake up all devices (Telegram, Discord) with a single click and complete local re-execution tasks.

One of the hottest projects in the AI ​​Agent community recently isClawdbot(The Little Claw Robot, whose mascot is a space lobster 🦞), is an open-source project created by renowned developer Peter Steinberger (@steipete, former founder of PSPDFKit). This trend is consistent with...Antigravity Agent Autonomous R&D SystemWhile they share similarities, Clawdbot targets personal schedules and end-user productivity.

From the first commit on November 24, 2025, to January 27, 2026, GitHub has surged to [number missing].51k stars、6.1k forksDevelopment is very active (there have been commits almost every day for the past week). Why is it so popular? In short: it truly enables large models to...”work”It's about doing things, not just chatting.

Today we'll break down this project in its entirety to help you quickly understand Clawdbot's origins, core architecture, why it became so popular, and the consequences that follow.”Sweet troubles”。

What problem did it actually solve?

Current AI assistants can be roughly divided into two categories:

  • Cloud-based chat(ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude Web Version): Strong reasoning ability, but poor long-term memory, and it's difficult to actually control your computer and account.
  • Mobile/PC native assistant(Siri, Xiao Ai, Google Assistant): They can do some things, but their models are weak, their ecosystems are closed, and their privacy is a concern.

Clawdbot combines the advantages of both: using the most advanced cutting-edge models (Claude Opus 4.5, GPT series, Ollam native models are recommended) to give you...Complete toolchainIt can operate your real computer and account, andAll self-hosted, local preferredData remains on the device.

The most killer experience is: you directly inTelegram、WhatsApp、Discord、Slack、Signal、iMessageYou can chat with it within your existing chat software. No need to open a new app.

The core architecture is clear at a glance.

Clawdbot employs a very clear modular design:

Gateway

The sole control center is bound to the local loopback mechanism by default.ws://127.0.0.1:18789,Responsible:

  • WebSocket connections for all clients
  • Integration of various chat channels
  • Tool call distribution
  • Session management, memory, scheduled tasks, Webhook
  • Canvas Visual Workspacehttp://...:18793/__clawdbot__/canvas/）

Node

The platform-specific runtime is responsible for operations that require local permissions, such as screen recording, camera access, notifications, and shell commands on macOS.

Channels

The input/output bridge currently supports 50+ platforms, including WhatsApp (QR code login), Telegram (Bot Token), Discord, Slack, Signal, iMessage (macOS only), Matrix, Zalo, and more.

Tools & Skills

The actual work:

  • built-inBrowser automation (dedicated Chrome CDP), Canvas visual workspace, system operation, scheduled tasks, etc.
  • ScalableInstall skills written by others through ClawdHub (Community Skills Hub), and it also supports AI-generated skills and hot-loading.
  • Secure isolationThe main session (your own) has full host permissions; group chats/stranger sessions are forced to use the Docker sandbox (strict whitelist/blacklist).

Model support

Anthropic (Opus 4.5 is highly recommended), OpenAI, local Ollama, etc., support routing and failover.

Workflow

The entire process was incredibly elegant:

你 → 在ChatBox发消息 → Channel → Gateway → AI 思考 + 工具调用 → 执行（主机或沙箱） → 回复到你ChatBox

Get started quickly 

1
2
3
4
5

# 需要 Node.js ≥ 22
npm install -g clawdbot@latest

# 启动向导，安装成系统服务
clawdbot onboard --install-daemon

The guide will lead you step by step:

  1. Configuration Model API Key
  2. Log in via chat (scan QR code or enter Token).
  3. Grant macOS the necessary permissions (screen recording, etc.)
  4. Create your first workspace

A few minutes later, you can @ it in any linked chat application.

Why did it become so popular so quickly? And why did problems arise?

The cause of fire

  • Install with one command+ Very capable (cleaning emails, flight check-in, browser operation, calendar management)
  • “Siri in the future”The feelingActive, voice wake-up, multi-channel, real-time canvas
  • Peter's personal influence+ Publicly available development logs using Claude for rapid development
  • Community outbreakDiscord has thousands of users, ClawHub is a skills marketplace, and various tutorials are available.

Classic Open Source Dilemma

But its rapid rise to fame also brought problems:

  • Non-technical usersSeeing a tweet/video and thinking it's cool → impulsively installing it → encountering complex configurations, bugs, and permission issues → various complaints.
  • security researchersSeeing a popular project with 50k+ stars → Scanning for vulnerabilities → Discovering someone misconfigured Tailscale/port forwarding, exposing the Gateway → Reporting and requesting a bug bounty.
  • Expectation MisalignmentMany people use it as a mature commercial product, ignoring the fact that it's a beta project less than 3 months old, mainly maintained by Peter (plus community PR), with donations barely enough to buy a Mac Mini.

Peter recently tweeted a complaint: his free hobby projects are being treated like products from big companies, and he's receiving a ton of enterprise-level requirements and security reports. The pressure from private messages and emails is immense.

The community response was very heartwarming: almost all the public replies were encouraging.”Keep going”“Noise shielding”“Someone is willing to sponsor the hardware.”。

Official security alert

From the documentation and recent commits:

  • Gateways are only bound to the local loopback interface by default; direct binding to the public network is strictly prohibited.
  • Remote access uses Tailscale + password authentication
  • Strangers DM Default”Pairing mode”Manual approval is required.
  • Non-master session forced Docker sandbox
  • Runclawdbot doctorCheck risk configuration

Some insights for open-source AI projects in 2026

  1. Viral growth is a double-edged swordThis brings a massive number of users, but also a massive amount of support pressure.
  2. The greater the ability, the greater the risk.Full system permissions are great, but misconfiguration can have serious consequences.
  3. Even the most prominent document warnings are often ignored by many people.(Especially the demo is so cool)
  4. Single-person maintainer faces extremely high burnout riskCommunity contribution, security audit, clear labeling”beta version; non-technical users should install with caution.”It can provide relief, but it doesn't address the root cause.

For those interested in playing (a technical background is recommended).

  • storehouse：https://github.com/clawdbot/clawdbot
  • document：https://docs.clawd.bot
  • Discord community：https://discord.gg/clawd(Very active)

It's recommended to test on a clean machine first, preferably using Anthropic Opus 4.5. Read the security page first. If you encounter any issues, please submit an issue or pull request in a friendly manner.

This lobster is still young, but it has already made many people rethink.”What should a personal AI assistant look like?”。

Would you prefer to run a local agent with full host privileges, or would you rather have a strict sandbox? Feel free to leave a comment and share your thoughts! 🦞
